column1: # maritime_routes.csv — curated Silk Road maritime polylines

column2: each a coast-following polyline of waypoints chosen so

column1: # the rendered line stays in water (no land-crossings) and passes through the
# major attested ancient harbours of the Periplus Maris Erythraei (1st c. CE)
# and the medieval Indian-Ocean / South-China-Sea sea lanes (Hansen 2012;
# Each waypoint is tagged with its modern name so reviewers can audit the choice.
# Routes are drawn as straight-line (PlateCarree) polylines in order listed.
# Density is high enough that no segment crosses land.
#   med  — Eastern->Western Mediterranean coastal route from Levant to Cadiz.
#   sea  — Indian Ocean Maritime Silk Road
# DO NOT HAND-EDIT silently — keep order column monotonically increasing integers.
# Note: route through Aegean goes E of Peloponnese via Cape Sounion to avoid
# drawing over the Peloponnese peninsula. N. African waypoints are pushed
# ~0.8-1.0 deg north of the coastline (which lies at 35.7-37.3 deg N).

column5: off Cape Sounion (SE Attica — avoids Peloponnese)
off Cape Matapan (S. tip of Peloponnese)
off Calabria / Cape Rizzuto (avoids toe of Italy)
off Cape Spartivento (S. tip of Calabria)
Carthage / Tunis offing (coast at ~37.0 N — stay north)
Cap Bon offing (N. of cape at 37.1 N)
Bejaia offing (coast at 36.75 N — stay north)
Algiers offing (coast at 36.77 N — stay north)
Oran offing (Oran city at 35.70 N — stay north)

column1: # Indian Ocean Maritime Silk Road (Levant -> Java)
# Levant -> Red Sea: coastal waypoints stay offshore of Sinai/Gaza.
# Aden -> Cana: dips south into Gulf of Aden (~12.5-13 N) to avoid Yemen
# coast (which rises from 12.8 N at Aden to ~14.5 N at Mukalla).
# Sayhut -> Khor Rori: intermediate waypoint at Ras Fartak to avoid Dhofar.

column5: Gulf of Aden (clear of Yemen coast — Yemen at ~13.3 N here)
Gulf of Aden mid (Yemen coast at ~13.6 N here)
off Mukalla (Yemen coast at ~14.5 N — stay south)
off Ras Fartak / W. Dhofar (avoids headland)
